html:
The role of a certified building design company is being expanded in the planning and building law to also include oversight during the execution of building projects. The term "certified building design company" is being changed to "building assessor." If a building assessor is used, the building authority will not review matters that fall within the building assessor's responsibilities. The types of new construction projects that can be covered by a building assessor's responsibilities will be specified in regulations issued under the authority of the law. The role of the expert is being strengthened. For certain building projects, expert oversight will be mandatory. The requirements for the control plan are being expanded to specify what the controls should cover and how they should be carried out. The control plan is being divided into two separate plans: one for general controls and one for waste management. A person responsible for quality control may not oversee the execution of work that they themselves have designed. The rules regarding technical consultations are being clarified regarding the documents required for the consultation and what the consultation should cover. The responsibilities of the building owner for design and execution are being clarified. The meaning of "workplace safety" is being strengthened. It is being clarified that a final inspection does not preclude later interventions. Installations that are essential for load-bearing capacity, stability, and durability must be maintained so that they always fulfill their intended purpose. The building authority may, through a control order, require a building owner or tenant to ensure that such maintenance requirements are met. It is being clarified that certification activities may involve the exercise of public authority, and that such decisions may be appealed. The proposal in this regard is that administrative tasks involving the exercise of public authority may also be delegated to foreign bodies. Therefore, the decision of the Parliament should be made in accordance with the procedure prescribed in Chapter 10, Section 6, paragraph 2 of the Instrument of Government. The proposed amendments to the law will come into force on July 1, 2026.

Proposal 2025/26:172

Government Proposal: The Riksdag (Swedish Parliament) adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Planning and Building Act (2010:900).

Proposal 2025/26:172 2 Proposed amendment to the Planning and Building Act (2010:900)

This proposal concerns the Planning and Building Act (2010:900) and stipulates the following:

  1. Sections 9 Chapter 67, 69, and 87 shall be repealed.
  2. The heading immediately preceding section 9 Chapter 67 shall be removed.
  3. The current sections 1 Chapter 5 and 6, and 10 Chapter 12 and 13 shall be renumbered as 1 Chapter 12 and 13, and 10 Chapter 11 b and 11 c, respectively.
  4. Sections 1 Chapter 1, 8 Chapter 14 and 25, 9 Chapter 68, 86, and 105, 10 Chapter 1, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 11, the new 11 b and 11 c sections, 14, 18, 19, 23, 23 a, 24, 27–32, and 34, 11 Chapter 1, 18, 19, 27, 34–37, 40, and 46, 13 Chapter 2 and 8, 16 Chapter 6, 9, and 11, the heading of 1 Chapter, and the headings immediately preceding 10 Chapter 5, 11 Chapter 34, and 16 Chapter 9 shall have the following wording.
  5. Twenty-seven new sections shall be introduced: 1 Chapter 5–11, 10 Chapter 5 a–5 c, 8 a, 11 a–13 e, and 40, 11 Chapter 17 a, and 13 Chapter 3 a and 3 b, and new headings shall be introduced immediately preceding 9 Chapter 68, 10 Chapter 8 a, 12, and 13, 11 Chapter 17 a, and 13 Chapter 3 a, with the following wording.

Current Wording | Proposed Wording ---|--- 1 Chapter. Purpose, content, and application | 1 Chapter. The purpose, content, and application of the Act. 1 § | 1 § In this Act, there are provisions regarding the planning of land and water, and construction. The provisions aim to, with due regard for individual freedom, promote social development with equal and good living conditions, and a good and sustainable living environment for people in today's society and for future generations. | In this Act, there are provisions regarding the planning of land and water, and the construction, maintenance, and inspection of buildings. The provisions aim to, with due regard for individual freedom, promote social development with equal and good living conditions, and a good and sustainable living environment for people in today's society and for future generations. 5 § | 5 § In this Act, a certification body or inspection body is an organization that has been accredited for the purpose of certifying or inspecting in accordance with the European Parliament and Council Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 of 9 July 2008 on accreditation and market surveillance relating to the marketing of products and repealing Regulation (EEC) No 339/93 and Section 5 of the Act (2011:791) on accreditation and technical control. | 6 § | 6 § In this Act, a certificate means a document or other document that shows that a certain quality or competence required by this Act is met and has been issued by:

  1. a certification body, or
  2. according to corresponding provisions in another country within the European Union or the European Economic Area. |

7 § | 7 § In this Act, a building inspector is an undertaking that:

  1. has a quality management system,
  2. has the appropriate competence, special expertise, and experience necessary to meet the requirements that a building must be suitable for its purpose, accessible, usable, and have essential technical properties, and
  3. can demonstrate its quality management system and its competence, expertise, and experience with a certificate. |

8 § | 8 § In this Act, a control supervisor is a natural person who:

  1. has the appropriate competence, knowledge, and experience necessary to ensure that a building measure, demolition measure, or land measure is carried out correctly,
  2. can demonstrate their competence, knowledge, and experience with a certificate, and
  3. has an independent position in relation to the person carrying out the measure to be inspected. |

9 § | 9 § In this Act, an expert is a natural person who:

  1. has the appropriate competence, special expertise, and experience necessary to control measures within their field of expertise, and
  2. can demonstrate their competence, expertise, and experience with a certificate. |

10 § | 10 § In this Act, a functional inspector is an expert who has the special expertise and experience necessary to inspect the function of a technical device or system in accordance with the Act. | 11 § | 11 § The government or the authority that the government designates may delegate to a certification body or an inspection body the following tasks:

  1. certification, and
  2. inspection to ensure that regulations issued pursuant to Section 2, 5, or 6 of Chapter 16 are followed, and that the requirements of European Parliament and Council Regulation (EU) 2016/424 of 9 March 2016 on cableway installations and repealing Directive 2000/9/EC are met. |

8 Chapter 14 § 2 | A building shall be maintained in good condition and maintained so that its design and the technical properties referred to in Section 4 are essentially preserved. The maintenance shall be adapted to the character of the surroundings and the value of the building from a historical, cultural, environmental, and artistic perspective.
tldr: New regulations for construction are proposed to come into effect on July 1, 2026. The purpose is to make the construction process safer and more transparent by changing roles and requirements for the inspection of buildings.
lattlast:
The government intends to amend the planning and building law. A significant change is that companies previously known as certified building design firms will now be called "building assessors." These building assessors will not only be responsible for designing buildings but will also be required to oversee the work on the construction site to ensure it is carried out correctly.

The regulations will also clarify what needs to be checked during a construction project. Previously, there was a single comprehensive control plan, but this will now be divided into two separate plans: one focusing on technical checks and the other on waste management. The requirements for experts to oversee specific aspects of the construction will also be increased.

For building owners and users, it will become increasingly important to maintain critical components, such as those that ensure the building's stability and safety. Failure to do so may result in the building authority, the local government body responsible for building permits, requiring inspections.

The proposal also clarifies the rules regarding what information must be included in technical consultations. It will also be easier to understand the responsibilities of the building owner (the client) to ensure that everything is done correctly from start to finish. All of these changes are planned to come into effect on July 1, 2026.
